content.problemStatement:
Chef is observing **asteroidListasteroidListasteroidList**, a sequence of asteroids moving through space.

Each asteroid has:


**Size** → given by its absolute value
**Direction** → positive (moving right), negative (moving left)
When two asteroids moving toward each other collide:


The **smaller** one explodes
If they are **equal**, both are destroyed
Asteroids moving in the **same direction** never collide
Chef wants to know the **final surviving asteroids** after all possible collisions.

Your task is to simulate these interactions and determine the final state.

Function Declaration

**Function Name**



resolveAsteroidCollisionsresolveAsteroidCollisionsresolveAsteroidCollisions



**Parameters**



asteroidListasteroidListasteroidList: A list of integers where each value represents an asteroid.



**Return Value**



Returns a list of integers representing the asteroids that survive after all collisions.
The order reflects their left-to-right positions.


Constraints
1≤T≤1051 \le T \le 10^51≤T≤105
2≤N≤1042 \le N \le 10^42≤N≤104
−1000≤asteroidList[i]≤1000-1000 \le asteroidList[i] \le 1000−1000≤asteroidList[i]≤1000
asteroidList[i]≠0asteroidList[i] \neq 0asteroidList[i]=0
Input Format
The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
For each test case:

The first line contains an integer NNN — the number of asteroids.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers representing the asteroidListasteroidListasteroidList.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing the asteroids remaining after all collisions.
The asteroids should be printed in order from left to right.
Each asteroid should be printed with its sign and size as in the input format.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains an integer N, the number of asteroids. The second line contains N space-separated non-zero integers representing the asteroidList.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the asteroids that remain after all possible collisions, in their original left-to-right order. Print the integers separated by a single space. If no asteroid survives, output an empty line.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: 4 and 3 move right, -5 moves left and destroys both, leaving -5.
Test case 2: 10 collides with -10 (both explode), 5 collides with -5 (both explode), 20 survives.
Test case 3: 1,2,3 move right, -4 destroys them, remaining -4 -3 -2.
Test case 4: Each left‑moving asteroid destroys the right‑moving ones it meets, leaving -10 -12 -14.
All asteroids move to the right, so no collisions occur.
5 and -5 destroy each other. 10 survives until -10 arrives; they are equal in size, so both explode. -2 is destroyed by 10 before -10 appears. No asteroid remains.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using a stack-based approach. We iterate through the asteroidList and for each asteroid, we check if it is moving towards the top of the stack (i.e., the asteroid at the top of the stack is moving in the opposite direction). If it is, we compare their sizes and handle the collision accordingly. If the asteroid at the top of the stack is smaller, we pop it from the stack. If they are of equal size, we pop the top asteroid from the stack and do not push the current asteroid. If the asteroid at the top of the stack is larger, we do not push the current asteroid. If the asteroid is moving in the same direction as the top of the stack, we simply push it to the stack.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T*N) total. The precomputation is done once for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the stack.
